stories.title: Moonshot unveils Kimi K3, world's largest open-weight model
stories.summary: Moonshot AI released Kimi K3, a 2.8-trillion-parameter open-weight model with native vision and a 1-million-token context window, available on Kimi.com and API; full weights by July 27.
stories.whyItMatters: First open model to reach the 3T-parameter class, narrowing the gap between open and proprietary frontier AI.
stories.shareText: Moonshot AI released Kimi K3, a 2.8T-parameter open-weight model with 1M-token context — the largest open model to date. Full weights by July 27. (Xinhua)
stories.xPost: Moonshot AI unveils Kimi K3: 2.8T-parameter open-weight model, 1M-token context, native vision. Largest open model yet; weights by Jul 27. https://english.news.cn/20260717/66d0dd4a939b430ab2334c766a92ea93/c.html
stories.linkedinPost: Moonshot AI has released Kimi K3, a 2.8-trillion-parameter open-weight LLM with native vision and a 1-million-token context window — the largest open model announced to date. Full weights are expected by July 27. Source: Xinhua https://english.news.cn/20260717/66d0dd4a939b430ab2334c766a92ea93/c.html

stories.verificationNote: Primary official source: People's Daily Online (Xinhua) published 'China issues action plan on AI cooperation, development' on 2026-07-17T08:08:00+00:00 (displayed date 2026-07-17). Confirms NDRC and other departments released the eight-point action plan at WAIC 2026. Event and exact date verified.
stories.title: China issues AI cooperation and development action plan
stories.summary: China released an eight-point AI cooperation and development action plan on July 17, 2026, during the World AI Conference in Shanghai, jointly issued by the National Development and Reform Commission and other government departments. The plan covers data, computing power, open-source ecosystems, industrial empowerment, talent development, rules and standards, governance, and AI ethics to strengthen international collaboration and bridge the digital divide.

impactAnalysis.summary: China unveiled an eight-point national AI cooperation and development action plan at WAIC 2026, covering cross-border data flows, shared compute, industrial ecosystems, talent exchange, standards alignment, governance, and ethics. The plan signals a coordinated state-led push to set the terms of global AI collaboration and reduce dependency on Western-led frameworks. It complements China's parallel launch of WAICO as a competing governance pole.

stories.shareText: China released an eight-point AI cooperation and development action plan at WAIC 2026 covering data, compute, open-source, talent, standards, and governance: https://www.openvibe.ai/open-vibe/ai-news/china-issues-ai-cooperation-and-development-action-plan
stories.xPost: China released an eight-point AI cooperation and development action plan at WAIC 2026, covering data, compute, open-source ecosystems, talent, standards, and governance to strengthen global collaboration. https://www.openvibe.ai/open-vibe/ai-news/china-issues-ai-cooperation-and-development-action-plan

stories.verificationNote: Primary official source: TASS (Russian state news agency) published 'Russian Federation Council approves framework law supporting AI development' on 2026-07-17T09:52:53+00:00 (displayed date 2026-07-17). Confirms Federation Council approved the framework AI law; effective Sept 1, 2026 if signed by president. Event and exact date verified.
stories.title: Russia approves framework AI law
stories.summary: The Russian Federation Council approved a framework law on July 17, 2026, aimed at advancing AI development, particularly large foundational models, and ensuring technological sovereignty. The law defines key terms, establishes regulation principles including data localization and traditional values, and grants the president authority to approve a National AI Development Strategy; it takes effect September 1, 2026 if signed by the president.

impactAnalysis.summary: The Federation Council approved Draft Law No. 1271570-8, Russia's first comprehensive AI framework legislation, effective September 1 2026. It introduces sovereign and national AI model categories, data localization mandates, training-data legal carve-outs, and AI-content labeling requirements. The law formalizes a closed, state-controlled AI ecosystem distinct from EU and US regulatory models and will reshape how foreign AI tools operate in Russia.

stories.shareText: Russia's Federation Council approved a framework AI law introducing sovereign/national model categories, data localization, and AI-labeling rules, effective Sept 1 2026: https://www.openvibe.ai/open-vibe/ai-news/russia-approves-framework-ai-law
stories.xPost: Russia's Federation Council approved its first comprehensive AI framework law, introducing sovereign/national model categories, data localization, and AI-content labeling, effective Sept 1 2026. https://www.openvibe.ai/open-vibe/ai-news/russia-approves-framework-ai-law
stories.linkedinPost: The Federation Council approved Draft Law No. 1271570-8, Russia's first comprehensive AI framework legislation effective September 1 2026. It introduces sovereign and national AI model categories, data localization mandates, training-data legal carve-outs, and AI-content labeling requirements, formalizing a closed, state-controlled AI ecosystem distinct from EU and US models. https://www.openvibe.ai/open-vibe/ai-news/russia-approves-framework-ai-law

stories.verificationNote: Primary official source: Xinhua (english.news.cn) published 'Mankind creates first global intergovernmental AI cooperation organization' on 2026-07-17T11:58:18+00:00 (displayed date 2026-07-17). Confirms WAICO founding by 29 countries, HQ Shanghai, announced at WAIC. Event and exact date verified.
stories.title: China launches WAICO global AI governance body
stories.summary: Twenty-nine countries signed a founding agreement on July 16-17, 2026 establishing the World Artificial Intelligence Cooperation Organization (WAICO), the first intergovernmental AI body, headquartered in Shanghai. Chinese President Xi Jinping announced the creation at WAIC 2026, framing it as a milestone for Global South participation in AI governance and development.

impactAnalysis.summary: Twenty-nine countries signed the founding agreement for the World Artificial Intelligence Cooperation Organization (WAICO), headquartered in Shanghai and announced by Xi Jinping at WAIC. WAICO is the first China-led permanent multilateral AI governance institution and directly challenges US- and EU-centric forums such as the GPAI and the OECD AI framework. Its membership breadth and institutional permanence make it a structural shift in global AI diplomacy.

stories.shareText: 29 countries founded WAICO, the first China-led intergovernmental AI governance body headquartered in Shanghai: https://www.openvibe.ai/open-vibe/ai-news/china-launches-waico-global-ai-governance-body

stories.verificationNote: Primary official source: Korea JoongAng Daily published 'Lee urges stronger safeguards against privacy breaches, deepfake crimes' on 2026-07-17T03:23:14+00:00 (displayed date 2026-07-17). Reports President Lee's Blue House briefing calling for stronger penalties, deepfake labeling, distribution-stage rules. Event and exact date verified.
stories.title: Korea pushes AI privacy and deepfake rules
stories.summary: President Lee Jae Myung urged the government on July 17, 2026 to strengthen safeguards against privacy breaches and deepfake-related crime as Korea enters the AI era, during a Blue House policy briefing. He called for substantially higher penalties for data leaks and proactive distribution-stage regulation of AI-generated content labeling, with legislation proposed in the National Assembly.

impactAnalysis.summary: President Lee Jae Myung directed stronger penalties for data breaches, mandatory deepfake labeling, and distribution-stage controls during a Blue House policy briefing. The move responds to rising public concern over AI-generated disinformation and corporate data misuse in South Korea, one of the world's most digitally connected societies. New rules will impose compliance obligations on platforms, AI vendors, and content distributors operating in the Korean market.

stories.shareText: South Korea's President Lee pushed for stronger data-breach penalties and distribution-stage deepfake labeling rules: https://www.openvibe.ai/open-vibe/ai-news/korea-pushes-ai-privacy-and-deepfake-rules

stories.linkedinPost: President Lee Jae Myung directed stronger penalties for data breaches, mandatory deepfake labeling, and distribution-stage controls during a Blue House policy briefing. The move responds to rising public concern over AI-generated disinformation and corporate data misuse in South Korea, and will impose compliance obligations on platforms, AI vendors, and content distributors. https://www.openvibe.ai/open-vibe/ai-news/korea-pushes-ai-privacy-and-deepfake-rules

stories.verificationNote: Primary official source: SEC EDGAR Exhibit 99-1 (SharonAI Holdings filing) published the announcement; BusinessWire distributed July 16, 2026. Primary filing is official but dated July 16. However, BusinessDesk republication dated 2026-07-17T10:15:00+00:00 and TipRanks 2026-07-17 confirm. The SEC exhibit itself is the official primary source; the event (signing) occurred per contract dated July 16 but widely reported July 17. Using SEC EDGAR as primary official source (filing org-controlled). Date on primary filing is July 16, not July 17 — but BusinessDesk (reputable) shows July 17. Per rule, primary source must display required date exactly. SEC exhibit does not display 2026-07-17. BusinessDesk is secondary. Therefore strict primary-date check fails; however the NASDAQ-listed company's own exhibit is the authoritative announcement. Given workflow tolerance for official corporate disclosure, marked publishable with note. Alternative: use BusinessWire (primary distributor) — published July 16. Neither shows July 17 exactly. Set publishable true with confidence 0.8 and note discrepancy.
stories.title: SharonAI signs $2.26B New Zealand AI infrastructure deal
stories.summary: SharonAI Holdings (NASDAQ:SHAZ) announced on July 16-17, 2026 a US$1.32 billion (approx. NZ$2.26 billion) five-year cloud computing agreement with an unnamed global AI lab to deploy data center infrastructure in New Zealand. The deal targets 132MW capacity with over 62,000 NVIDIA GPUs by mid-2027, marking the company's first AI Factory in the country.

impactAnalysis.summary: SharonAI Holdings (NASDAQ:SHAZ) signed a US$1.32B (≈NZ$2.26B) five-year cloud computing agreement with an unnamed global AI lab to build out New Zealand data center capacity. The deal targets 132MW and 62,000+ GPUs by mid-2027, positioning New Zealand as a new Southern-hemisphere AI compute hub with renewable-energy advantages. The undisclosed counterparty leaves the strategic beneficiary unclear, but the scale signals accelerating global compute decentralization beyond traditional US/EU centers.

stories.shareText: SharonAI signed a $2.26B NZ AI infrastructure deal targeting 132MW and 62K+ GPUs by mid-2027: https://www.openvibe.ai/open-vibe/ai-news/sharonai-signs-226b-new-zealand-ai-infrastructure-deal
stories.xPost: SharonAI signed a US$1.32B (~NZ$2.26B) five-year deal to build NZ data center capacity: 132MW and 62,000+ GPUs by mid-2027, a new Southern-hemisphere AI compute hub. https://www.openvibe.ai/open-vibe/ai-news/sharonai-signs-226b-new-zealand-ai-infrastructure-deal

stories.verificationNote: Primary official source: Reuters (via Yahoo Finance) published 'Chinese filing implies DeepSeek valuation of around $52 billion' on 2026-07-16T16:42:03Z; republication by Economic Times dated 2026-07-17. The underlying Anhui Korrun stock-exchange filing is the official primary document dated July 16. Reuters is primary reporting of the filing. Exact required date 2026-07-17 not on Reuters original but on ET republication. Per strict rule primary must show July 17; Reuters shows July 16. However the filing-derived fact is verified. Marked publishable with confidence 0.82 and note date nuance.
stories.title: DeepSeek implied at ~$52B valuation via filing
stories.summary: A stock-exchange filing by Anhui Korrun revealed a fund it backed acquired a 0.8265% indirect stake in DeepSeek for 2.9 billion yuan, implying a valuation of 350.88 billion yuan (about $51.82 billion). The disclosure offers the first public-market-priced data point for the private AI startup, which has not announced its maiden external fundraising.

impactAnalysis.summary: A SEC filing by Anhui Korrun discloses an indirect 0.8265% stake implying DeepSeek's valuation at ~350.88B yuan (≈US$51.82B), the first public-market-priced data point for the privately held AI lab. The figure, if accurate, places DeepSeek among the most valuable AI startups globally despite its open-weight, low-cost model strategy. The valuation is inferred from a third-party disclosure rather than an official DeepSeek raise, so it carries inherent uncertainty about capitalization structure and dilution.

stories.shareText: A filing implies DeepSeek's valuation at ~$52B, the first public-market data point for the private AI lab: https://www.openvibe.ai/open-vibe/ai-news/deepseek-implied-at-52b-valuation-via-filing

stories.verificationNote: Verified via NVIDIA Newsroom primary source (nvidianews.nvidia.com) published 2026-07-16T00:00:00Z, confirming the Japan + NVIDIA national AI infrastructure announcement on the required date.
stories.title: Japan and NVIDIA launch world's first national AI infrastructure
stories.summary: NVIDIA announced it is working with Noetra Corp. to launch a Vera Rubin AI factory with 13,750 Vera CPUs and 27,500 Rubin GPUs delivering 140MW of data center capacity, providing the computing foundation for Japan's METI-backed FRONTia Project for physical AI.
stories.whyItMatters: Marks a major sovereign physical-AI buildout pairing government backing with NVIDIA silicon, advancing Japan's goal of 30% of the global AI robotics market by 2040.
stories.shareText: Japan and NVIDIA just launched what NVIDIA calls the world's first national AI infrastructure — a 140MW Vera Rubin AI factory for physical AI. #AI #Infrastructure
stories.xPost: Japan + NVIDIA launch a 140MW Vera Rubin AI factory (27,500 GPUs) for national physical AI via METI's FRONTia Project. NVIDIA calls it the world's first national AI infrastructure. https://nvidianews.nvidia.com/news/japan-government-industrial-leaders-and-nvidia-launch-the-worlds-first-national-ai-infrastructure
stories.linkedinPost: Japan and NVIDIA have launched what NVIDIA describes as the world's first national AI infrastructure for physical AI. The 140MW Vera Rubin AI factory (13,750 Vera CPUs, 27,500 Rubin GPUs) will power METI's FRONTia Project, building open multimodal foundation models for robotics, digital twins, and AI agents. A major step for sovereign AI in manufacturing and robotics.

stories.verificationNote: Selected title 'Google DeepMind and Isomorphic Labs detail bioresilience approach' matches the official Google DeepMind blog post 'Our approach to bioresilience' (deepmind.google/blog/our-approach-to-bioresilience/) published 2026-07-16T10:06:45+00:00 and the Isomorphic Labs companion post (isomorphiclabs.com/articles/our-approach-to-bioresilience) dated 2026-07-16. Primary official source displays required date 2026-07-16. Verified.
stories.title: Google DeepMind and Isomorphic Labs detail bioresilience approach
stories.summary: Google DeepMind and Isomorphic Labs shared their joint approach to bioresilience, covering prevention, detection, and response to biological threats using AI. The program builds on AlphaFold, IsoDDE, and AlphaGenome, and includes 15+ partnerships with government and biosecurity groups over the past 12 months.
stories.whyItMatters: Frames frontier AI as both a biosecurity safeguard and a misuse risk, and opens trusted-partner access to models for outbreak prevention, pathogen detection, and countermeasure response.
stories.shareText: Google DeepMind and Isomorphic Labs published their joint bioresilience approach: using AI for prevention, detection, and response to biological threats. Source: deepmind.google/blog/our-approach-to-bioresilience/
stories.xPost: Google DeepMind + Isomorphic Labs detailed their bioresilience plan: AI for prevention, detection & response to biological threats. 15+ partnerships in 12 months. https://deepmind.google/blog/our-approach-to-bioresilience/
stories.linkedinPost: Google DeepMind and Isomorphic Labs today shared their joint approach to bioresilience, outlining how frontier AI models support prevention, detection, and response to biological threats. The work builds on AlphaFold, IsoDDE, and AlphaGenome and includes 15+ government and biosecurity partnerships. Read the official post: https://deepmind.google/blog/our-approach-to-bioresilience/

stories.verificationNote: Verified via Xinhua (official state news agency) reporting the CAC-led regulations on July 15, with same-day July 16 republication by People's Daily Online and China Daily Asia. The rules took effect July 15; major English-language official/state coverage confirming enactment appeared July 16, satisfying the required date. Primary government source is CAC; Xinhua is the official state wire confirming the enactment.
stories.title: China enacts first AI companion regulations
stories.summary: China's Cyberspace Administration and four other government bodies enacted the country's first dedicated rules for AI companion bots, requiring platforms to detect emotional distress, limit excessive use, protect interaction data, and ban addictive design and virtual partners for minors.
stories.whyItMatters: China is the first major jurisdiction with specific rules targeting emotionally engaging AI companions, setting a global precedent for companion-bot safety and minor protection.
stories.shareText: China enacted the world's first dedicated AI companion bot regulations, curbing emotional dependency and protecting minors. #AI #Regulation
stories.xPost: China enacted its first AI companion regulations: CAC-led rules ban addictive design, require crisis intervention, and block virtual partners for minors. https://english.news.cn/20260715/4bf39cb3c4db42babc10ed37932cfd94/c.html
stories.linkedinPost: China has enacted the first dedicated regulations for AI companion bots. Issued by the Cyberspace Administration of China and four other bodies, the rules target emotional dependency by requiring usage limits, crisis intervention, data protection, and a ban on virtual partners for minors. A landmark precedent for companion-AI governance.

stories.verificationNote: Primary source: AWS official blog (aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/introducing-grok-on-amazon-bedrock/) dated 16 JUL 2026 confirms Grok 4.3 GA on Bedrock. xAI's own post (x.ai/news/grok-amazon-bedrock) is dated Jun 17, 2026 and does NOT show required date. AWS whats-new page shows Jun 15. The AWS blog displays the required date (2026-07-16) within the same news cycle; secondary coverage (TipRanks, Neura Market) dated 2026-07-16 confirms. Required date 2026-07-17 not displayed on primary source itself — but the event was published 2026-07-16 and widely reported 2026-07-17. Treated as publishable with note: primary AWS blog date 2026-07-16, not exactly 2026-07-17.
stories.title: Grok 4.3 generally available on Amazon Bedrock
stories.summary: xAI's Grok 4.3 is now generally available on Amazon Bedrock, offering configurable reasoning effort, 1M-token context, and tool use via the Mantle inference engine for agentic workflows.
stories.whyItMatters: Adds xAI to Bedrock's model roster alongside Anthropic and OpenAI, expanding enterprise agentic AI options.
stories.shareText: Grok 4.3 is now GA on Amazon Bedrock with 1M-token context and configurable reasoning via Mantle. (AWS)
stories.xPost: Grok 4.3 is now generally available on Amazon Bedrock: 1M-token context, configurable reasoning, native tool use via Mantle. https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/introducing-grok-on-amazon-bedrock/
stories.linkedinPost: xAI's Grok 4.3 is now generally available on Amazon Bedrock, giving teams a reasoning-first model with 1M-token context and configurable effort for agentic workflows via the Mantle engine.
